title: The ï؟½Seaboltï؟½sï؟½ Volunteers
content:
آ آ آ آ They towed the Seabolt down the stream,
آ آ آ آ And through the harbourï؟½s mouth;
آ آ آ آ She spread her wings and sailed away
آ آ آ آ To seek the sunny South.

آ آ آ آ But, ah! she met with storm on storm
آ آ آ آ Ere half her course had run;
آ آ آ آ And all her masts were torn away,
آ آ آ آ And all her boats save one.

آ آ آ آ The good old ship had settled far
آ آ آ آ Beneath her cargo line,
آ آ آ آ Her riven sides were drinking deep
آ آ آ آ The draughts of ocean brine.

آ آ آ آ There gathered round the only boat
آ آ آ آ The women pale with fear,
آ آ آ آ And trembling little ones, who clung
آ آ آ آ To those who held them dear.

آ آ آ آ Then spoke the captain, brave and true,
آ آ آ آ His voice rose oï؟½er the roar;
آ آ آ آ ï؟½The boat will save us all but five,
آ آ آ آ She cannot float with more!ï؟½

آ آ آ آ And backward from the side he steppedï؟½
آ آ آ آ (He had been born at sea)
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Now who will seek in oceanï؟½s depths
آ آ آ آ A sailorï؟½s grave with me?ï؟½

آ آ آ آ Then up there stepped a merchant stout,
آ آ آ آ His face was brown and tan:
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Iï؟½ll volunteer to stay on board,
آ آ آ آ For Iï؟½m an Englishman!ï؟½

آ آ آ آ Then spoke a gallant gentleman,
آ آ آ آ A lover of romance:
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Remain I for the ladiesï؟½ sake,
آ آ آ آ For Iï؟½m a son of France!ï؟½

آ آ آ آ And next there spoke a Highlander:
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Go search the wide world round,
آ آ آ آ Youï؟½ll find no spot where on the earth
آ آ آ آ A Scotsman is not found!ï؟½

آ آ آ آ And then there spoke a lad to whom
آ آ آ آ Killarneyï؟½s lakes were dear:
آ آ آ آ ï؟½It wonï؟½t be said that Ireland found
آ آ آ آ No place of honour here!ï؟½

آ آ آ آ The boat pushed from the vesselï؟½s side
آ آ آ آ Amid the ringing cheers;
آ آ آ آ And now beneath Old Ocean sleep
آ آ آ آ The Seaboltï؟½s volunteers.
meaning:
Henry Lawson's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "The ï؟½Seaboltï؟½sï؟½ Volunteers"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
